name: fetch
description: HTTP fetch — retrieve and convert web pages to markdown. No secrets. Useful for reading docs, blog posts, GitHub READMEs, etc. without leaving the session.

install.alt_install: uvx mcp-server-fetch  (recommended — no global pip install)

notes: Prefer uvx over pip install — keeps the server isolated in its own venv per invocation.
This server respects robots.txt by default. Override with --ignore-robots-txt as an arg if you need to.
It does NOT execute JavaScript; single-page apps may return empty content. For JS rendering, use a browser-based MCP server instead (playwright-based ones exist but are heavier).
Built-in Claude Code WebFetch covers most cases; this server is useful when you want the MCP protocol specifically (for tool use in subagents, etc.).
